# Upload a single page ad-hoc

This guide shows how to upload documents without creating a `.text2confl.yml` configuration file - useful for quick
one-off uploads.

## Upload a directory

Pass all required parameters on the command line:

```shell
text2confl upload --docs . \
  --confluence-url https://wiki.example.org \
  --user bob \
  --password secret_password \
  --space DOCS \
  --parent "Autogenerated docs"
```

## Upload a single file

Point `--docs` at a specific file instead of a directory:

```shell
text2confl upload --docs ./my-file.md \
  --confluence-url https://wiki.example.org \
  --user bob \
  --password secret_password \
  --space DOCS
```

## See also

- [Authenticate with Confluence](./authenticate.md) - credential options including environment variables
- [Configuration reference](../reference/configuration.adoc) - full list of upload options
